methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $8.6B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.0B - $7.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Cohere
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $7B

private.name: Hugging Face
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $4.5B

private.name: ElevenLabs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $3B+

private.name: Hive AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $2B

private.name: Labelbox
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Wayve is a Series D artificial intelligence and software company founded by Amar Shah and Alex Kendall. The valuation analysis triangulates across a peer universe of 9 comparable private companies and 1 public comparable (Palantir Technologies), reflecting the concentrated state of mature AI & software venture financings.

Two methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchored to Wayve's last post-money valuation of $8.6B, applies a typical 1.5x step-up multiplier for progression beyond Series D, yielding $12.9B. Peer-median analysis examines five same-stage, same-sector comparables—Cohere ($7.0B), Hugging Face ($4.5B), ElevenLabs ($3.0B), Hive AI ($2.0B), and Labelbox ($1.0B)—producing a median of $3.0B. The peer range spans $1.0B to $7.0B, highlighting material dispersion in how the market prices AI software at this stage.

Triangulating both methods yields a valuation range of $2.5B (floor) to $14.8B (ceiling), with a median of $8.0B. Wayve's current $8.6B valuation sits at the high end of peer medians but aligns with the triangulated midpoint. Key uncertainties include the timing and probability of next-stage progression, which drives the stage-progression method's 1.5x multiplier, and the relevance of peer companies whose business models and monetization trajectories may diverge materially from Wayve's autonomous vehicle focus.
